title: Youth for COP 2026 Program Opens Applications: Join a Global Climate Learning Journey for Young Changemakers
summary: The third edition of the Youth for COP Program is officially live, offering young people around the world an exciting opportunity to explore global climate conversations, climate diplomacy, and international negotiations connected to the Conference of Parties (COP). Designed as a dynamic climate education and engagement platform, Youth for COP 2026 empowers young people with the knowledge, skills, networks, and confidence needed to meaningfully engage in climate action and policy discussions at local, regional, and global levels. The program combines expert-led learning sessions, interactive workshops, online modules, simulations, and networking opportunities to help participants understand how global climate systems work and how youth voices can contribute to shaping climate solutions. Most importantly, the program is completely free of cost and provides selected participants with certificates and opportunities to explore and engage with COP31 and the wider global climate ecosystem. What is Youth for COP 2026? Youth for COP is an international climate engagement initiative created to bridge the gap between young people and global climate policymaking. Since launching in 2023, the program has evolved into a vibrant youth platform bringing together climate advocates, students, young professionals, researchers, and emerging leaders from across the world. Now entering its third edition, Youth for COP continues to expand its impact by helping participants: Understand international climate negotiations Learn how COP processes work Build climate communication skills Develop digital advocacy capacity Connect with a global network of young climate leaders The initiative is especially valuable for young people interested in climate policy, sustainability, environmental justice, advocacy, diplomacy, youth participation, and global governance. Why Youth for COP Matters Climate change is one of the defining challenges of our generation, yet many young people still struggle to access spaces where climate policies and negotiations are discussed. Youth for COP addresses this gap by creating accessible learning opportunities that: Simplify complex climate negotiations Build climate literacy Encourage youth participation Strengthen youth voices in climate spaces Foster collaboration across countries and cultures The program recognizes that meaningful climate action requires informed and empowered young leaders who can engage critically and constructively in global conversations. What Participants Will Learn The program offers a broad and immersive learning experience focused on international climate governance and practical advocacy skills. Key Learning Areas Include: 1. Understanding COP and Climate Negotiations Participants will learn: What COP is How UN climate negotiations work How climate agreements are shaped The role of governments, civil society, and youth in climate discussions 2. Climate Policy and Global Governance The program explores: International climate frameworks Climate justice Adaptation and mitigation Loss and damage discussions Global climate priorities 3. Communication and Digital Advocacy Participants will develop: Public communication skills Climate storytelling techniques Digital advocacy strategies Social media engagement skills 4. Youth Leadership and Networking The initiative also focuses on: Leadership development Collaborative learning Cross-cultural engagement Networking with climate-focused youth globally Program Structure and Flow The Youth for COP 2026 program follows a structured learning journey designed to progressively build participants’ understanding and engagement. Step 1: Registration and Application Interested participants must: Register for the program Complete the application form Because seats are limited, participants are selected through a competitive process. Step 2: Knowledge Building Selected participants gain access to: Expert-led sessions Online learning modules Climate policy discussions Educational resources This stage provides foundational understanding of climate negotiations and COP processes. Step 3: Interactive Workshops Participants engage in: Simulations Interactive activities Group discussions Collaborative exercises These workshops help participants apply their knowledge in practical ways. Step 4: Activities and Practical Engagement Participants complete: Short assignments Advocacy activities Communication tasks Learning reflections These exercises strengthen real-world engagement and application. Step 5: Certification Participants who successfully complete the program receive: Certificate of participation Certificate of appreciation The certification recognizes participants’ engagement and commitment to climate learning and advocacy. Benefits of Joining Youth for COP 2026 Participants in the program gain access to multiple educational and networking opportunities. 1. Learn Directly From Experts The program features: Climate experts Policy professionals Researchers Youth leaders Climate advocates Participants gain valuable insights into real-world climate negotiations and global policy discussions. 2. Access Global Opportunities Participants can explore: International climate opportunities Youth climate networks Advocacy platforms Engagement opportunities linked to COP31 3. Build Valuable Skills The program strengthens: Communication skills Leadership abilities Digital advocacy Critical thinking Climate literacy 4. Join a Global Community Participants become part of an international network of young people passionate about climate action and sustainability. Program Reach and Impact Since its launch, Youth for COP has built an impressive global community. Program Statistics Over 3,600 registered participants Participants from 48+ countries More than 40 speakers Collaboration with 25 youth organizations Over 80 climate blogs published More than 350 social media posts created These numbers reflect the growing influence and global reach of the initiative. Participant Voices and Climate Storytelling One of the program’s unique features is its emphasis on youth storytelling and lived experiences. Participants publish blogs exploring: Climate vulnerability Indigenous knowledge Climate justice Sustainability Fashion and climate Community resilience Adaptation and survival These stories amplify youth voices from regions heavily impacted by climate change and contribute to global climate dialogue. Who Can Apply? The program welcomes applications from young people interested in: Climate action Sustainability Climate governance Environmental advocacy Policy engagement Youth leadership Importantly: No prior climate experience is required Students and working professionals can apply Participants from different academic and professional backgrounds are encouraged Program Format The Youth for COP program is designed to be flexible and accessible. Program Features Online learning format Interactive workshops Virtual engagement Flexible participation for students and professionals This structure allows participants from different countries and time zones to engage effectively. Cost of Participation One of the most attractive aspects of the program is that it is: Completely free of cost This ensures accessibility for participants from diverse economic backgrounds. Will Participants Attend COP? While participation in the program does not automatically guarantee attendance at COP, the initiative helps participants: Understand global climate spaces Build climate engagement skills Access opportunities connected to COP31 and related climate platforms How to Apply Interested applicants should: Register through the official platform Complete the application form Submit all required information before seats are filled Because participation is limited, early applications are highly encouraged. Register HERE Why You Should Apply Youth for COP 2026 is more than an educational program—it is a platform for young people to become informed climate advocates and active participants in shaping global climate conversations. Whether you are new to climate advocacy or already active in sustainability work, the program offers a valuable opportunity to: Expand your knowledge Strengthen your voice Build international networks Develop practical advocacy skills Engage with global climate conversations in meaningful ways For young changemakers passionate about climate action, Youth for COP 2026 could be a transformative experience. For more information about this opportunity, visit the official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post Youth for COP 2026 Program Opens Applications: Join a Global Climate Learning Journey for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026: Australia Opens Up to $100,000 Funding for Hands-On STEM Learning Projects
summary: Organizations across Australia now have an exciting opportunity to secure significant funding through the Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026, a national initiative designed to strengthen creativity, innovation, and hands-on STEM learning among young people. The program provides grants ranging from $20,000 to $100,000 to support community-based STEM projects that help students and youth under 18 years old develop practical skills in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics through inquiry-based and experiential learning activities. The initiative particularly focuses on increasing participation among groups that have historically been underrepresented in STEM education and careers, ensuring more young people across Australia gain access to meaningful STEM opportunities. About the Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026 The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants are part of Australia’s broader effort to promote innovation, scientific literacy, and technological capability among young people. The grants support organizations that deliver engaging STEM activities outside traditional classroom settings. These projects encourage experimentation, creativity, problem-solving, design thinking, engineering skills, and entrepreneurship through interactive and hands-on experiences. Rather than relying solely on theoretical instruction, the program emphasizes practical learning approaches where students actively build, create, test, and explore STEM concepts. Funding Amount Available Successful applicants can receive: Grants from $20,000 to $100,000 The funding is intended to help organizations design and deliver impactful STEM engagement initiatives for young people under 18 years of age. Application Deadline Applications for the Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026 are currently open. Closing Date Wednesday, 17 June 2026 Time: 5:00 PM AEST (Australian Eastern Standard Time) Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early and ensure all required supporting documentation is complete before submission. Purpose of the Program The Maker Projects Grants aim to: Foster creativity and inquiry-based learning Improve STEM knowledge and practical skills Increase access to STEM opportunities Encourage innovation and entrepreneurial thinking Inspire young people to pursue future STEM studies and careers The program recognizes the growing importance of STEM education in preparing future generations for evolving industries, digital transformation, and technological advancement. Key Objectives of the Grant Opportunity The program has several important objectives designed to improve STEM participation and educational outcomes among young Australians. 1. Deliver Hands-On STEM Learning Projects should allow students and youth under 18 years old to: Experiment with technologies Explore engineering and programming concepts Participate in maker activities Develop practical STEM skills The focus is on active participation and experiential learning. 2. Encourage Creativity and Innovation The program supports initiatives that: Promote creative problem-solving Encourage entrepreneurial thinking Build confidence in STEM-related activities Inspire innovation through practical engagement 3. Increase Interest in STEM Careers Projects should help participants: Discover future career pathways Build interest in STEM subjects Understand real-world applications of technology and science This long-term approach helps strengthen Australia’s future STEM workforce. 4. Improve Accessibility and Inclusion A major focus of the grants is ensuring STEM opportunities are accessible to young people who may face barriers to participation. The program particularly supports projects serving: Regional communities Rural areas Remote communities Economically disadvantaged groups Priority Groups Supported by the Program The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants place special emphasis on increasing participation among groups historically underrepresented in STEM. These include: 1. Women and Girls Projects that encourage female participation in STEM learning and leadership are strongly supported. 2. First Nations Peoples Programs that engage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people are encouraged. 3. Young People in Regional, Rural, and Remote Areas The grants seek to improve STEM access outside major metropolitan areas. 4. Culturally and Linguistically Diverse (CALD) Communities Projects supporting inclusive STEM participation among multicultural communities are welcomed. 5. People with Disability Inclusive STEM learning opportunities for young people with disabilities are prioritized. 6. Young People from Low Socio-Economic or Educationally Disadvantaged Backgrounds The program supports initiatives that reduce barriers and create equal learning opportunities. Expected Outcomes of the Program The Maker Projects Grants aim to generate long-term positive outcomes for young people and communities across Australia. Anticipated Outcomes Include: Increased STEM knowledge and practical skills Greater participation in STEM-related activities Improved innovation and entrepreneurial thinking Increased accessibility to quality STEM learning experiences Stronger engagement with science and technology pathways Long-term development of inquiry-based learning skills Who Can Apply? Organizations may apply if they meet the eligibility criteria outlined in the grant guidelines. Applicants must demonstrate: Eligibility as an approved entity An eligible STEM project Eligible project expenditure The complete eligibility requirements are detailed in the official grant opportunity guidelines. Eligible Projects Projects funded under this program should: Deliver community-based STEM engagement activities Target students and youth under 18 years old Use hands-on and inquiry-based learning approaches Encourage experimentation and practical skill development Examples may include: STEM workshops Coding and robotics programs Maker spaces Engineering challenges Innovation labs Technology camps Science engagement events How to Apply Organizations interested in applying should carefully prepare before starting the application process. Application Process Step 1: Review the Grant Guidelines Applicants should thoroughly read and understand: Eligibility rules Funding conditions Assessment criteria Project requirements Step 2: Create or Log Into the Online Portal Applications must be submitted through the official online grants portal. Step 3: Complete the Application Applicants should provide: Detailed project information Supporting evidence Budget details Impact goals Organizational information Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline Applications must be fully completed before submission. Incomplete or incorrect applications may not be considered, and mistakes cannot be corrected after submission. Apply HERE How Applications Are Assessed Applications will be evaluated based on: Alignment with program objectives Potential impact on young people Accessibility and inclusion Project quality and feasibility Community engagement Value for money Strong applications will clearly demonstrate how the project will create meaningful STEM learning outcomes. Why These Grants Matter STEM education is increasingly important in today’s rapidly evolving world. However, many young people still face barriers to accessing quality STEM learning opportunities. The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants help bridge this gap by supporting organizations that create accessible, engaging, and practical STEM experiences for diverse communities across Australia. By investing in youth STEM engagement today, the program contributes to: Future workforce development Innovation ecosystems Educational equity Economic growth Community empowerment Final Thoughts The Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026 offer a valuable opportunity for organizations passionate about STEM education to expand their impact and empower the next generation of innovators, creators, and problem-solvers. With funding of up to $100,000 available, eligible organizations can design transformative projects that inspire curiosity, creativity, and long-term engagement in science and technology. Organizations working to make STEM education more inclusive, practical, and accessible are strongly encouraged to apply before the June 17, 2026 deadline. For more information about the opportunity, visit the official website Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post Maker Projects Community STEM Engagement Grants 2026: Australia Opens Up to $100,000 Funding for Hands-On STEM Learning Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Black Girls Defense Fund – Resistance and Resilience Grant 2026 (Up to $20,000 Funding Opportunity for Southern US Organizations)
summary: The Black Girls Defense Fund has officially opened applications for its Resistance and Resilience Grant , a powerful funding opportunity aimed at strengthening organizations that defend, protect, and advocate for Black girls, women, and femme-identifying youth in the Southern United States . This grant supports community-led initiatives working at the frontlines of justice, safety, health equity, and systemic change. It is designed to fund organizations that are actively addressing structural issues such as gender-based violence, reproductive health inequities, adultification, dehumanization, and harms caused by carceral systems. Grant funding ranges from $5,000 to $20,000 , with a strong emphasis on supporting grassroots movements and organizations deeply rooted in their communities. About the Black Girls Defense Fund The Black Girls Defense Fund is an initiative of the Southern Black Girls and Women’s Consortium. It exists to strengthen and protect movements advancing the rights, dignity, and safety of Black girls and women across the Southern United States. The fund focuses on: Community protection and safety Reproductive and maternal health justice Ending gender-based violence Restorative justice approaches Challenging systemic dehumanization and adultification The initiative prioritizes organizations that are led by and directly accountable to the communities they serve. About the Grant The Resistance and Resilience Grant is a flexible funding opportunity designed to support both new and existing initiatives. It provides general operating support to help organizations sustain and expand their work in areas including: Advocacy campaigns Community programs Justice reform initiatives Health and safety programs Youth empowerment efforts The goal is to strengthen long-term capacity for organizations working with Black girls, women, and femme-identifying youth. Funding Amount Minimum: $5,000 Maximum: $20,000 This funding can be used for general operating support, allowing organizations flexibility in how resources are allocated. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for this grant, applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Organizational Requirements Must be a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization , OR Have a fiscal sponsor with a nonprofit organization Budget Requirement Organization must have an annual budget under $1 million Geographic Focus Applicants must operate in at least one of the following Southern U.S. states: Alabama Arkansas Florida Georgia Kentucky Louisiana Mississippi North Carolina South Carolina Tennessee Virginia West Virginia Texas Eligible Focus Areas Organizations must be working on initiatives that address at least one of the following: Gender-based violence prevention and response Reproductive and maternal health justice Justice system and carceral system reform Restorative justice practices Safety and wellbeing of Black girls and women Advocacy against adultification and dehumanization Key Responsibilities of Funded Work Funded organizations are expected to demonstrate impact through: Program Implementation Designing or continuing initiatives that support Black girls, women, and femme youth Implementing advocacy or direct service programs Strengthening community safety systems Community Engagement Engaging directly with affected communities Incorporating feedback from stakeholders Ensuring culturally responsive programming Impact Measurement Defining clear outcomes and success indicators Tracking program effectiveness and community impact Reporting on progress and learnings Application Requirements Applicants must complete the JustFund Common Application™ and submit supporting documents. Required Documents W9 form Proof of 501(c)(3) status OR fiscal sponsorship agreement Most recent Form 990 Organizational budget Application Questions Overview Applicants will be required to respond to detailed questions covering: 1. Program Design and Impact Description of the initiative Activities and implementation plan Expected outcomes and metrics Organizational background and leadership history 2. Community Engagement Strategy How community input is collected and integrated How programs are sustained or expanded Media engagement preferences 3. Resource Allocation Use of grant funding Partnerships and collaborations Funding gaps and sustainability strategy Additional support needs beyond funding Key Dates and Timeline Application Opens: May 5, 2026 (9:00 AM EST) Information Session: May 7, 2026 (7:00 PM EST) Application Deadline: May 14, 2026 (8:00 PM EST) Funding Decisions Announced: June 24, 2026 Information Session A live workshop will be held to guide applicants through the process and answer questions. Details: Date: May 7, 2026 Time: 7:00 PM Eastern Time (US Canada) How to Apply Click here for more, to register and to apply Important notes: Account activation may take up to 2 days Applicants are encouraged to apply at least 3 days before the deadline Late submissions may not be considered Why This Grant Matters This funding opportunity plays a critical role in strengthening grassroots movements that protect and empower Black girls and women in the Southern United States. It provides essential unrestricted support that allows organizations to: Sustain vital advocacy work Respond to community needs in real time Expand justice-focused initiatives Strengthen leadership in marginalized communities For more global grant opportunities Click here The post Black Girls Defense Fund – Resistance and Resilience Grant 2026 (Up to $20,000 Funding Opportunity for Southern US Organizations) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: YLabs Youth Researcher Opportunity in Nigeria 2026 – Remote Field-Based Research Role for Early-Career Professionals
summary: YLabs is inviting applications from Nigeria-based early-career professionals for an exciting Youth Researcher opportunity focused on participatory and community-based research. This short-term contractor role offers candidates the chance to contribute to meaningful youth-centered projects while gaining hands-on experience in ethnographic research, design research, and field engagement. The organization is looking for individuals who are deeply connected to their communities and capable of gathering rich, context-driven insights through direct interaction with young people and local stakeholders. This role is ideal for emerging professionals interested in: Community engagement Participatory research Youth development Social impact Human-centered design Public health and development research Successful applicants will support field research activities across Nigeria while collaborating remotely with a global multidisciplinary team. About YLabs YLabs is a globally recognized design and research organization dedicated to amplifying youth power, agency, and opportunity through innovative technologies and inclusive research methods. Founded in 2014 at the Harvard Innovation Lab, YLabs partners directly with young people to co-create digital products and services that address pressing social challenges affecting youth globally. The organization works across more than 20 countries and focuses on: Youth health and wellness Economic opportunity Climate resilience Digital innovation Behavioral science Adolescent development YLabs combines expertise in: Human-centered design Epidemiology Digital product design Community engagement Research and behavioral science The organization is headquartered in Rwanda and the United States, with a globally distributed workforce. About the Youth Researcher Role The Youth Researcher role is designed for individuals with foundational experience in research and field engagement who can work directly within communities to uncover lived experiences, patterns, challenges, and opportunities. Researchers will support participatory research activities by: Conducting interviews Leading group discussions Carrying out observations Documenting field insights Participating in synthesis sessions The role requires a strong understanding of local contexts and the ability to respectfully engage individuals from diverse backgrounds. Contract Duration and Work Structure Employment Type Independent Contractor Work Location Nigeria (Remote with field-based activities) Contract Timeline Expected to run from May to Mid-July 2026 Work Style Primarily field-based Some remote collaboration required Key Responsibilities Selected Youth Researchers will support a range of participatory research activities. Core Duties Include: 1. Field Research Coordination Plan and coordinate field visits Manage schedules and local logistics Navigate community engagement processes 2. Conducting Research Activities Carry out one-on-one interviews Facilitate group discussions Conduct direct field observations 3. Community Engagement Build trust with youth and community stakeholders Create safe and respectful environments for discussion Encourage honest and open participation 4. Documentation and Insight Gathering Capture detailed field notes Record reflections and observations using structured tools Identify behavioral and environmental patterns 5. Data Reflection and Synthesis Participate in debrief sessions Surface emerging themes and recurring issues Help identify opportunities and tensions within communities 6. Ethical and Safe Research Practice Follow safeguarding and child protection guidelines Handle sensitive information responsibly Adhere to ethical research standards Ideal Candidate Profile YLabs is looking for candidates who are: Community-centered Curious and empathetic Strong listeners Respectful and culturally aware Reflective and open to learning Applicants should be able to connect authentically with people while maintaining professionalism and ethical standards. Required Qualifications and Experience Minimum Requirements Candidates should have: At least 2 years of experience in: Research Monitoring and evaluation Community engagement Design research Related development or social impact work This experience may come from: Education Professional work Training Community or lived experience Required Skills Applicants must demonstrate: Strong communication skills in English and relevant local languages Ability to conduct interviews and document findings clearly Strong observation and note-taking skills Comfort interacting with diverse community groups Ability to adapt to dynamic field environments Familiarity with digital tools and online collaboration platforms Confidential handling of information Understanding of ethical research and safeguarding principles Additional Advantage Experience working with: Major funders Philanthropic organizations Development organizations across Africa will be considered an added advantage. Compensation and Benefits Pay Rate Up to $11 per hour Compensation is based on experience Payment Method Monthly bank transfer payments YLabs follows Project Fair compensation principles, ensuring equitable pay standards based on work value and regional benchmarks. Eligibility Criteria This opportunity is specifically open to: Candidates based in Nigeria Individuals with strong understanding of local cultures and languages Applicants comfortable conducting in-person field engagement YLabs strongly encourages applications from: Women Persons living with disabilities Application Requirements Interested applicants must submit: Required Documents Resume/CV Short video introduction (1–2 minutes) What to Include in the Video The video should: Introduce your background Explain your connection to your community Discuss a community issue you care about Describe how you would investigate and understand the issue Explain: Who you would speak with How you would engage people What methods you would use to analyze information How to Submit the Video Applicants must: Upload the video to: Google Drive OR YouTube Ensure access settings are: “Anyone with the link can view” OR “Public” Paste the video link into the application form Important: Ensure the link works before submission Videos with restricted access cannot be reviewed YLabs emphasizes that production quality is not important. Applicants are encouraged to focus on: Clarity Organization Authenticity Application Deadline The deadline to apply is: 15 May 2026 Late or incomplete applications may not be considered. Click here for more and to apply Selection Process YLabs will: Review all submitted applications Contact shortlisted candidates during the hiring process Conduct background checks and criminal record clearances before final hiring decisions All applicant information will remain confidential. Equal Opportunity Statement YLabs is committed to promoting equity, inclusion, and belonging in the workplace. The organization welcomes applicants from all: Cultural backgrounds Educational backgrounds Socio-economic groups Gender identities Physical abilities Age groups YLabs maintains a strict non-discrimination policy. Why This Opportunity Matters This opportunity provides emerging researchers in Nigeria with: Real-world field research experience Exposure to global development work Experience in participatory and youth-centered design Collaboration with international experts Skills in ethnography and community engagement It is especially valuable for individuals seeking careers in: Development research Human-centered design Social innovation Public health Youth programming International development Click here for more global job opportunities The post YLabs Youth Researcher Opportunity in Nigeria 2026 – Remote Field-Based Research Role for Early-Career Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: University of Greenwich Postdoctoral Research Fellow Opportunity 2026 in the UK: Fully Engage in Global Research on Gender-Based Violence, Food Systems, and Social Justice
summary: The University of Greenwich has announced applications for an exciting Post Doctoral Research Fellow position within its internationally recognized Natural Resources Institute (NRI). This opportunity offers researchers the chance to contribute to groundbreaking work exploring Gender-Based Violence (GBV) within food systems through innovative, interdisciplinary, and socially impactful research. The position is based at the Medway Campus in the United Kingdom and provides a unique opportunity for researchers passionate about gender studies, food systems, participatory research, social justice, and sustainable development. The successful candidate will join a globally respected research environment dedicated to addressing some of the world’s most pressing development challenges through collaborative and evidence-based approaches. Applications close on May 20, 2026 . About the University of Greenwich and the Natural Resources Institute (NRI) University of Greenwich is widely recognized as one of the United Kingdom’s leading institutions for impactful and socially engaged research. The university has built a strong reputation for advancing innovative research that contributes directly to policy development, sustainable development, and community transformation around the world. Within the university, the Natural Resources Institute (NRI) serves as a specialist research institute focused on: Food systems Sustainable agriculture Livelihoods and institutions Social justice Climate resilience International development NRI collaborates extensively with partners across the Global South and supports evidence-driven initiatives that influence both policy and practice globally. This postdoctoral role will be embedded within NRI’s dynamic interdisciplinary research environment, where researchers work closely with governments, civil society organizations, academic institutions, and local communities. About the Research Project Food systems around the world are increasingly recognized as spaces where gender inequalities strongly shape: Labour conditions Access to resources Decision-making power Economic opportunities Exposure to violence Despite growing global attention to gender equality, Gender-Based Violence (GBV) within food systems remains significantly under-researched. This postdoctoral fellowship forms part of a pioneering transdisciplinary initiative that examines GBV across: Agricultural labour systems Food value chains Contexts of food insecurity Rural and marginalized communities The research adopts: Feminist approaches Intersectional analysis Decolonial perspectives Creative and embodied methodologies Audio-visual research techniques The project aims to generate new evidence and creative outputs capable of informing: Public policy Development programming Advocacy initiatives Public awareness Academic scholarship Role Overview The University of Greenwich is seeking a highly motivated Postdoctoral Research Fellow with expertise in one or more of the following areas: Gender studies Food systems research Participatory methodologies Creative research methods Social justice research Development studies The selected researcher will contribute to several key activities throughout the project. Main Responsibilities 1. Co-Producing Research Outputs The researcher will help produce: Audio-visual research materials Written reports Academic publications Creative dissemination materials 2. Stakeholder Collaboration The role involves co-designing research materials and outputs with: Community stakeholders International partners Civil society actors Research collaborators 3. International Research Collaboration The successful candidate will work closely with project partners in: Colombia Nigeria This provides valuable international exposure and collaborative research experience across multiple contexts. 4. Dissemination and Knowledge Sharing The researcher will support the communication of findings to: Policymakers Academic audiences Civil society organizations Community actors International development stakeholders 5. Academic Contributions The role includes contributing to high-impact academic publications and interdisciplinary research outputs. Salary and Contract Details Salary The annual salary for this position ranges from: £38,784 to £44,746 per annum The exact salary will depend on qualifications and experience. Contract Type Fixed-term contract Duration: Until March 31, 2027 Work Location Medway Campus United Kingdom Why This Opportunity Stands Out This position is particularly valuable for researchers seeking to: Build an international academic career Conduct socially impactful research Engage with interdisciplinary methodologies Collaborate with Global South partners Influence policy and practice Work within feminist and decolonial research frameworks The opportunity also offers exposure to innovative creative research approaches, including audio-visual methodologies that are increasingly important in participatory and community-centered research. Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion Commitment The University of Greenwich strongly emphasizes equality, diversity, and inclusion in its recruitment and workplace culture. The university particularly encourages applications from underrepresented groups, including: Black communities Asian communities Minority ethnic groups Disabled individuals LGBT+ applicants The institution is committed to: Mental health support Inclusive recruitment practices Reasonable workplace adjustments Diverse talent management Employee wellbeing The university also promotes a supportive and inclusive environment for researchers from diverse backgrounds and experiences. How to Apply Interested applicants must apply through the official University of Greenwich vacancies portal. Applicants are required to: Complete the online application form Provide all required supporting statements Submit the application before the deadline Important note: Attached CVs and personal statements will not be reviewed at the initial application stage unless the required online sections are completed properly. Applicants are therefore encouraged to carefully complete every section of the online application portal. Important Dates Application Release Date May 6, 2026 Application Deadline May 20, 2026 Interview Date To be confirmed Official Application Links Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post University of Greenwich Postdoctoral Research Fellow Opportunity 2026 in the UK: Fully Engage in Global Research on Gender-Based Violence, Food Systems, and Social Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Restless Development Uganda Consultancy Opportunity 2026: Remote Financial Capacity Building Consultancy for Youth-Led Organizations Funded by the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab
summary: Restless Development is inviting qualified consultants and consultancy teams to apply for an important remote consultancy opportunity focused on strengthening the financial management systems and institutional capacity of youth-led organizations in Uganda. The consultancy forms part of the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab – Power of the Collective (AU-EU YVL-PoC), a major four-year initiative co-funded by the European Union to support youth participation, governance, and inclusive development across Africa and Europe. This opportunity is ideal for experienced finance, accounting, compliance, and NGO management professionals with strong expertise in capacity building, financial systems strengthening, and organizational development for grassroots and youth-led organizations. The consultancy will run for three months and offers a total budget of UGX 16,000,000 / €4,000 , inclusive of taxes and professional fees. Applications close on Thursday, May 21, 2026, at 5:00 PM (E.A.T) . About Restless Development Restless Development Official Website is a globally recognized youth-led development agency committed to supporting young people to create a just, equitable, and sustainable world. Since 1985, the organization has worked with thousands of young people globally to: Promote youth leadership Strengthen civic engagement Advance climate justice Improve livelihoods and education Promote sexual and reproductive health rights Encourage inclusive governance Restless Development believes that young people are powerful agents of change and should be at the forefront of addressing local and global challenges. The organization operates across multiple countries and works closely with youth-led organizations, governments, civil society actors, and development partners. About the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab – Power of the Collective The AU-EU Youth Voices Lab – Power of the Collective (AU-EU YVL-PoC) is a four-year programme designed to build more inclusive societies in Africa and Europe by ensuring that youth voices are integrated into governance and policy-making processes. The programme focuses on: Youth participation in governance Civic engagement Leadership development Advocacy skills Institutional responsiveness to youth concerns The initiative is implemented through a consortium of respected international organizations, including: Oxfam Restless Development Search for Common Ground Youthmakers Hub Makesense Africa The programme equips young people with the tools and skills needed to advocate for their priorities at: Local levels National levels Regional levels Continental platforms It also works with institutions and policymakers to strengthen youth inclusion in governance systems. Background of the Consultancy As part of the programme implementation, 15 youth-led organizations were selected through a competitive funding process by the Youth Advisory Board and Restless Development. Following selection: Due diligence assessments were conducted Organizational capacity gaps were identified Capacity Building Plans (CBPs) were jointly developed with grantees The assessments identified several institutional and financial management challenges requiring external technical support. Some of the key identified needs include: Weak internal control systems Limited financial management tools Gaps in procurement systems Inadequate human resource policies Weak compliance structures Organizational governance challenges This consultancy therefore seeks to provide technical assistance and tailored capacity-building support to selected grantees. Objective of the Consultancy The consultant will help strengthen the institutional and financial management capacity of prioritized youth-led organizations funded under the programme. The main goal is to ensure that grantees: Improve financial accountability Strengthen compliance systems Develop sustainable internal control mechanisms Enhance institutional resilience Meet donor financial reporting requirements Key Responsibilities of the Consultant The selected consultant or consultancy team will undertake several important tasks. Review Existing Organizational Systems The consultant will review: Due diligence reports Capacity Building Plans (CBPs) Organizational structures Financial policies Administrative systems Human resource procedures Procurement systems This assessment will help determine the specific support required by each organization. Deliver Tailored Capacity Building Training The consultant will: Develop a training schedule Conduct customized training sessions Strengthen accountability mechanisms Build sustainable financial management systems The training should support the development of: Democratic organizations Legitimate governance structures Sustainable institutional systems Transparent financial processes Support Development of Internal Policies The consultant will work directly with grantees to co-create and strengthen: Financial policies Procurement policies Human resource policies Internal control systems Risk management frameworks This support aims to improve organizational governance and operational efficiency. Consultancy Duration and Budget Duration Three months Engagement Mode Fully remote/virtual Budget UGX 16,000,000 Equivalent to approximately €4,000 Inclusive of taxes and professional fees Required Qualifications Applicants should possess strong expertise in finance, compliance, and organizational development. Educational Qualifications Candidates should have: An advanced degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field CPA or ACCA certification is preferred Professional Experience Applicants must demonstrate: At least 5–7 years of relevant professional experience Experience managing EU-funded projects Experience working with international NGOs Proven expertise in financial management and compliance Previous experience conducting organizational capacity-building assignments Required Skills and Expertise The ideal consultant should have: Strong knowledge of internal controls and risk management Experience supporting grassroots organizations Change management expertise Familiarity with youth-led programming Experience working with NGOs and civil society organizations in Africa Strong facilitation and training skills Safeguarding Commitment Restless Development places strong emphasis on safeguarding and the protection of: Children Young people Vulnerable adults The organization expects all consultants, staff, volunteers, and partners to comply fully with safeguarding standards and policies to ensure communities are protected from harm. Why This Consultancy Matters This consultancy provides a unique opportunity to contribute directly to strengthening youth-led organizations and improving institutional sustainability across Africa. By supporting financial systems and governance structures, the selected consultant will help youth organizations: Become more resilient Improve donor compliance Strengthen transparency Scale their impact Sustain long-term development initiatives The consultancy also offers valuable experience working within an international consortium funded by the European Union. How to Apply Interested consultants should submit the following documents: Required Application Documents Technical proposal outlining methodology and approach Financial proposal with a detailed budget in: Uganda Shillings (UGX) or Euros (€) Relevant portfolio and references CV(s) of consultancy team members Identification of a team lead Important Dates Deadline for Inquiries Monday, May 18, 2026 at 12:00 PM (E.A.T) Application Deadline Thursday, May 21, 2026 at 5:00 PM (E.A.T) Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is highly encouraged. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Restless Development Uganda Consultancy Opportunity 2026: Remote Financial Capacity Building Consultancy for Youth-Led Organizations Funded by the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Women in Tech Accelerator 2026: Up to USD 600K in Grants for Women-Led Startups Across Africa, the Middle East, and Pakistan
summary: Women entrepreneurs across Africa, the Middle East, and Pakistan now have an incredible opportunity to scale their businesses, access funding, and connect with global industry leaders through the Women in Tech Accelerator 2026. Powered by the Standard Chartered Foundation in partnership with Village Capital and local implementing partners, this accelerator program is designed to support women-led, tech-enabled startups that are creating measurable social and economic impact in their communities. The accelerator goes beyond traditional startup support by combining funding opportunities, mentorship, investment-readiness training, peer networking, and personalized business growth support. Since its launch, the initiative has already empowered dozens of women founders and distributed hundreds of thousands of dollars in grants to businesses solving real-world challenges. For ambitious women entrepreneurs seeking to grow sustainable and impactful startups, the Women in Tech Accelerator 2026 represents a major opportunity to access both financial and strategic support. About the Women in Tech Accelerator 2026 The Women in Tech Accelerator is a specialized entrepreneurship program focused on strengthening women-led startups that use technology to drive impact and innovation. The program supports founders operating in sectors where technology can create meaningful solutions for communities, industries, and economies. Through expert guidance and market-specific support systems, participating startups receive the tools they need to scale sustainably and become investment-ready. Village Capital works closely with local implementation partners in participating countries to ensure founders receive support tailored to their specific business environment and market realities. The accelerator emphasizes: Business growth Leadership development Access to funding Investment readiness Market expansion Strategic networking Impact of the Program So Far The Women in Tech Accelerator has already demonstrated significant impact in its early years. Achievements from Previous Cohorts In its first year alone: More than USD 550,000 in grants was distributed 21 women-led startups received funding and support The 2025 cohort collectively achieved: Nearly 16,000 new customers reached More than 430 jobs created Over USD 2.7 million in increased revenue These results highlight the accelerator’s role in strengthening women entrepreneurship ecosystems while contributing to economic growth and job creation. Funding Available in 2026 For the 2026 cohort, the program plans to distribute: More than USD 600,000 in grant funding This funding will be shared across startups selected from 12 participating markets. Eligible Countries for the 2026 Cohort Women-led startups from the following countries are eligible to apply: Bahrain Botswana Egypt Ghana Kenya Nigeria Pakistan Saudi Arabia South Africa United Arab Emirates (UAE) Uganda Zambia Special Funding Notes Funding for startups in: Saudi Arabia South Africa is provided by the respective local Standard Chartered Banks rather than the Standard Chartered Foundation. Who Should Apply? The Women in Tech Accelerator is looking for: Women founders Women-led startups Tech-enabled businesses Startups creating measurable impact Applicants should be operating businesses that use innovation and technology to address important economic, social, or environmental challenges. The program is particularly suitable for founders seeking: Growth support Investment readiness Funding opportunities Regional and international exposure Strategic mentorship Benefits of the Women in Tech Accelerator Selected startups will gain access to a wide range of growth and development opportunities. 1. Grant Funding Opportunities Participants may access portions of the: USD 600K+ annual grant funding pool This funding can help startups: Expand operations Develop products Hire staff Enter new markets Improve technology infrastructure 2. Personalized Growth Support Each startup receives: Tailored development plans Customized business support Strategic guidance based on specific needs and challenges This personalized approach ensures founders receive practical assistance aligned with their stage of growth. 3. Investment Readiness Training Participants will undergo training focused on: Strengthening business models Financial planning Pitch preparation Growth strategy development Investor engagement This support helps founders become more competitive when seeking future investment opportunities. 4. Access to Mentors and Industry Leaders The accelerator creates opportunities to connect with: Experienced entrepreneurs Sector experts Investors Business mentors Innovation leaders These relationships can open doors to partnerships, funding, and long-term collaboration. 5. Strong Peer Networks Participants become part of a broader community of: Women entrepreneurs Innovators Startup founders Ecosystem builders Peer learning and collaboration are key aspects of the accelerator experience. Local Implementation Partners The Women in Tech Accelerator works with several local partners to deliver country-specific support and programming. Some of the implementation partners include: INNOVentures Global Companies Creating Change (C3) Innovation Village BongoHive WomHub These organizations help ensure that support is locally relevant and responsive to the needs of entrepreneurs in each market. Why This Accelerator Matters Women entrepreneurs continue to face barriers in accessing: Capital Business networks Mentorship Investment opportunities The Women in Tech Accelerator addresses these challenges directly by creating a platform where women founders can gain the resources, confidence, and support needed to grow successful businesses. By supporting women-led startups, the program also contributes to: Inclusive economic growth Job creation Innovation ecosystems Community development Sustainable impact How to Apply Interested founders should: Visit the official website Identify their local country partner Review country-specific requirements Complete and submit the application process For more information and to apply, visit the official website Applicants are encouraged to prepare: Business information Growth plans Impact metrics Startup details Founder profiles Early application is strongly recommended. Final Thoughts The Women in Tech Accelerator 2026 is more than a startup program—it is a transformational platform for women innovators who are building businesses that create meaningful impact. With access to grant funding, mentorship, investment-readiness training, and strategic networks, participating founders can significantly strengthen their businesses and position themselves for long-term success. For women entrepreneurs ready to scale innovative solutions across Africa, the Middle East, and Pakistan, this accelerator offers the support, visibility, and opportunities needed to thrive in today’s evolving business landscape. Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post Women in Tech Accelerator 2026: Up to USD 600K in Grants for Women-Led Startups Across Africa, the Middle East, and Pakistan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Senior/Lead Education Specialist (Foundational Literacy & Numeracy) in AI for LMICs – Fab AI Global Remote Opportunity 2026
summary: This is a high-impact recruitment opportunity for an experienced education specialist to join a mission-driven team working at the intersection of education, artificial intelligence, and global development. The role focuses on designing and leading pedagogical frameworks for AI evaluation systems that assess foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) in low- and middle-income country (LMIC) contexts. The organization is expanding its education team and is seeking either a Senior or Lead Education Specialist depending on experience. The successful candidate will play a central role in defining what high-quality learning looks like when AI systems are used to support early-grade reading and mathematics across diverse languages, scripts, and resource-constrained classrooms. This position is ideal for professionals who have deep, practical experience in foundational education systems and want to influence how AI technologies are evaluated for real-world classroom impact. About the Organization Fab AI is a not-for-profit organization dedicated to improving educational outcomes for learners who are currently underserved by global education systems. With nearly a decade of experience and a multidisciplinary team spread across 14 countries, the organization brings together educators, economists, data scientists, designers, and software engineers. The team operates across Europe and Sub-Saharan Africa and focuses exclusively on education innovation, particularly in low-resource and multilingual learning environments. Their mission is to ensure that emerging technologies, especially artificial intelligence, are designed and evaluated with strong pedagogical foundations. Job Purpose and Core Focus The primary purpose of this role is to lead the pedagogical design of AI benchmarks and evaluation frameworks for foundational literacy and numeracy in LMIC classrooms. The role ensures that AI systems used in education are assessed based on robust educational principles rather than purely technical performance metrics. Key focus areas include: Early-grade reading and mathematics instruction Multilingual and multicultural classroom environments Low-resource education systems Alignment between pedagogy and AI evaluation standards Key Responsibilities The selected candidate will contribute across multiple high-impact areas: 1. Pedagogical Design of AI Benchmarks Develop structured task taxonomies for AI evaluation in education Create assessment items and prompts aligned to early-grade curricula Design scoring rubrics that reflect real classroom learning outcomes Ensure benchmarks reflect diverse languages and writing systems 2. Expansion of Existing Evaluation Frameworks Extend current benchmarks covering pedagogy, SEND, visual mathematics, and African languages Strengthen foundational literacy and numeracy coverage within AI evaluation systems 3. Design of AI Evaluation Systems Build frameworks for evaluating both student-facing and teacher-facing AI tools Ensure evaluation methods reflect real educational challenges in LMIC classrooms 4. Classroom-Based Evidence Integration Participate in rapid-cycle evaluation studies in real-world school environments Translate classroom realities into measurable AI evaluation criteria 5. Stakeholder Collaboration Work closely with governments, education partners, developers, and teachers Support evidence synthesis to inform decision-making in education systems Candidate Profile and Requirements The ideal candidate will have extensive experience in foundational education systems, particularly in LMIC contexts. Essential Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Experience developing teaching and learning materials from design through implementation and adaptation Strong background in foundational literacy and numeracy assessment tools such as EGRA, EGMA, ASER, or UWEZO Deep understanding of systematic phonics across languages and literacy progression models Experience in multilingual education systems and language transition strategies Expertise in early mathematics instruction and classroom-based numeracy approaches Ability to work across multiple projects in dynamic environments Strong collaboration skills within diverse international teams A problem-solving mindset and openness to continuous learning Highly Desirable Experience Structured pedagogy and Teaching at the Right Level (TaRL) approaches Inclusive education design, including Universal Design for Learning (UDL) Experience in classrooms with limited resources, large student populations, or low teacher training capacity Curriculum design and alignment with government education systems Additional Valuable Experience Writing decodable texts for early reading instruction Teacher professional development and coaching in LMIC settings Familiarity with AI-enabled educational technologies such as adaptive learning systems Cross-cultural collaboration in education and development contexts Role Level and Compensation This position is available at two levels: Senior Level Salary range: £52,800 – £79,200 Lead Level Salary range: £67,200 – £100,800 Additional Geneva-based salary uplift may apply where relevant. Location and Working Model This is a remote-first role with global collaboration across Europe and Sub-Saharan Africa. To ensure effective collaboration, candidates must be available during core working hours: 10:00 to 17:00 Central European Time Applicants from any country are welcome, provided they can consistently overlap with these working hours. Work authorization is required for candidates based in locations such as Geneva or London where applicable. Expected Impact in First 6 Months A successful candidate will be expected to: Lead the development of at least one new FLN AI benchmark with full taxonomy and rubric design Contribute to at least one real-world AI evaluation cycle in an LMIC classroom setting Establish strong working relationships with partners, government stakeholders, and technical teams Application Process Applicants are required to submit: A CV A short expression of interest Responses to application questions Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Click here for more and here to apply. The final application deadline is 26 May 2026. Early applications are encouraged as interviews may begin before the deadline. Fore more global job opportunities Click here . title: Fully Funded CMU-Africa Innovation Hub Business Incubation Program 2026/2027 in Rwanda | Up to $50,000 Seed Funding for African Tech Startups
summary: African tech founders looking to scale innovative startups now have an exciting opportunity to accelerate their entrepreneurial journey through the CMU-Africa Innovation Hub Business Incubation Program 2026/2027. Organized by Carnegie Mellon University Africa (CMU-Africa), this prestigious fully funded incubation program is designed to support early-stage African technology startups with funding, mentorship, business advisory support, global networking opportunities, and investor connections. The 12-month incubation program is aimed at helping promising startups transform their proof-of-concept ideas into scalable, revenue-generating ventures capable of attracting additional investments and competing successfully in African and global markets. Hosted in Kigali, Rwanda, the CMU-Africa Innovation Hub continues to position itself as one of Africa’s leading innovation ecosystems for entrepreneurship, technology, and startup development. If you are an African entrepreneur building a scalable tech-enabled solution with strong market potential, this program could become the turning point for your startup. About the CMU-Africa Innovation Hub Business Incubation Program The CMU-Africa Innovation Hub Business Incubation Program is specifically designed for ambitious African tech founders who already have: A functional prototype Preliminary market validation A scalable business idea A clear understanding of the problem they are solving The program goes beyond traditional incubation support by helping startups strengthen their products, refine business models, gain investor readiness, access global networks, and position themselves for long-term growth. The overall goal is to ensure participating startups leave the incubation program with: A revenue-generating product Strong growth potential Improved operational structure Access to funding and partnerships Investment readiness for scaling Participants will also gain direct access to CMU-Africa’s extensive entrepreneurship ecosystem, including the globally recognized Swartz Center for Entrepreneurship at Carnegie Mellon University. Benefits of the CMU-Africa Innovation Hub Business Incubation Program 2026/2027 Selected startups will receive substantial financial, technical, and strategic support throughout the program. 1. Up to $50,000 USD in Seed Funding One of the biggest highlights of the program is access to up to $50,000 USD in seed funding provided by CMU-Africa. This funding is intended to help startups: Improve their products Expand operations Strengthen market entry Build teams Accelerate growth 2. Twelve Months of Intensive Advisory Support Participants will receive one year of targeted startup support covering critical business areas such as: Product development Business growth strategy Compliance and legal readiness Investment preparation Go-to-market planning Operational efficiency This structured support helps startups avoid common growth challenges while improving scalability and sustainability. 3. AWS Credits and Technical Support Selected startups will benefit from: $5,000 in AWS credits $1,500 in Amazon technical support These resources can help startups reduce cloud infrastructure costs while improving product development and technical operations. 4. Investor and Partnership Connections The program connects founders with: Angel investors Venture capital firms Strategic partners Innovation stakeholders in Africa and Pittsburgh This exposure creates opportunities for fundraising, partnerships, and international market expansion. 5. Access to CMU-Africa’s Innovation Ecosystem Participants become part of CMU-Africa’s growing entrepreneurial ecosystem across Africa. This includes: Networking opportunities Collaboration with innovators Exposure to African tech ecosystems Access to industry experts 6. Global Entrepreneurial Network Through Carnegie Mellon University’s Swartz Center for Entrepreneurship, startups gain access to global entrepreneurial communities and innovation networks. This international exposure can open doors to: Mentorship Partnerships International investors Research and innovation collaboration Program Timeline Applicants should carefully note the official timeline for the 2026/2027 cohort. Key Dates 30 April – 20 May 2026: Call for applications 5 and 8 May 2026: Information sessions 5 June 2026: Cohort confirmation 13 July 2026: Official program kick-off Application Deadline The deadline to apply is: 20 May 2026 Interested startups are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute technical challenges. Who Can Apply? The CMU-Africa Innovation Hub Business Incubation Program is open to African tech startups that meet specific eligibility requirements. Eligibility Criteria To qualify, startups must: 1. Be Less Than Two Years Old The startup should be relatively early-stage and less than two years old at the time of application. 2. Build a Scalable Tech-Enabled Solution Applicants must be working on technology-driven solutions addressing clear market opportunities in Africa. Examples may include solutions in: Fintech HealthTech EdTech AgriTech ClimateTech AI and data solutions Logistics Digital services 3. Have a Functional Prototype The startup must already have: A proof-of-concept prototype Early validation Initial traction or customer feedback A clear path toward profitability 4. Demonstrate Strong Market Understanding Applicants should show: Deep understanding of their market Clear business strategy Sustainable revenue model Scalability potential 5. Have At Least Two Committed Co-Founders The startup must be led by at least two committed co-founders willing to dedicate full-time effort to the business. 6. Be a Registered Company The startup should already be legally registered. Who Is NOT Eligible? Certain startups are not eligible for the incubation program. Applications will NOT be considered from: Marketplace businesses E-commerce middleman platforms Non-tech-enabled startups Late-stage startups with significant capital Startups without functional prototypes Startups that have participated in more than two similar programs in the last 12 months These restrictions ensure the program focuses on early-stage, innovation-driven startups that can benefit most from incubation support. Why African Founders Should Apply Africa’s startup ecosystem continues to grow rapidly, but many founders still struggle with: Access to funding Technical mentorship Investor readiness Product scaling Strategic partnerships The CMU-Africa Innovation Hub Business Incubation Program addresses these challenges directly by combining funding, mentorship, technical support, and international networks. This makes it one of the most valuable startup incubation opportunities currently available for African entrepreneurs. Participants gain not only financial support but also long-term strategic advantages that can significantly improve startup survival and scalability. How to Apply Interested startups can apply through the official application platform. APPLY HERE Applicants should prepare: Startup information Prototype details Business model explanation Team details Market validation evidence Growth strategy Pro Tip for Applicants The most competitive applications will clearly demonstrate: A real market problem Strong customer validation Scalability potential A committed founding team Sustainable revenue opportunities Avoid generic applications. Instead, show measurable traction, user feedback, and a strong understanding of your target market. A clear and realistic path to profitability can significantly strengthen your application. Click HERE to learn more about the CMU-Africa Innovation Hub Business Incubation Program 2026/2027 in Rwanda. Conclusion The CMU-Africa Innovation Hub Business Incubation Program 2026/2027 represents a major opportunity for African tech entrepreneurs seeking funding, mentorship, and global exposure. With up to $50,000 in seed funding, world-class advisory support, investor connections, and access to Carnegie Mellon University’s global innovation ecosystem, the program is designed to help African startups move from early-stage ideas to scalable ventures capable of creating lasting impact. Founders ready to build transformative technology solutions for Africa should strongly consider applying before the deadline. Discover more global Fellowship and Entrepreneurship opportunities on OFY. title: Jane Goodall Institute of Canada Youth Advisory Council 2026/2027: Fully Immersive Leadership Opportunity for Young Environmental Changemakers in Canada
summary: The Jane Goodall Institute of Canada is inviting passionate and impact-driven youth to apply for the 2026/2027 Youth Advisory Council (YAC), an exciting leadership and volunteer opportunity designed for young people who are committed to creating positive change for people, animals, and the environment. The Youth Advisory Council is one of the flagship youth engagement initiatives under the Jane Goodall Institute of Canada. Through this programme, selected young leaders will collaborate with peers across Canada to influence programming, co-design environmental and social impact projects, and contribute directly to conversations surrounding biodiversity loss, climate change, and environmental inequity. This opportunity is specifically designed for youth aged 18–30 who will reside in Canada throughout the programme term from July 2026 to June 2027. The initiative welcomes applicants from all immigration statuses, provided they are living in Canada during the entire fellowship period. About the Jane Goodall Institute of Canada Youth Advisory Council The Youth Advisory Council (YAC) serves as a platform for empowering young voices in environmental leadership and advocacy. The council ensures that youth perspectives and lived experiences remain central to the development and implementation of programs led by the Jane Goodall Institute of Canada. Participants will engage in: Leadership development Project management Environmental advocacy Collaborative programme design Youth engagement initiatives Community-centered environmental solutions National youth networking opportunities The YAC focuses on addressing three interconnected global crises: Biodiversity loss Climate change Environmental inequity Through collaborative action and innovative thinking, council members contribute to meaningful environmental and social change within Canada and beyond. Why the Youth Advisory Council Matters Young people are increasingly at the forefront of environmental and social justice movements globally. The Jane Goodall Institute of Canada recognizes the importance of empowering youth with the tools, networks, and opportunities needed to influence sustainable futures. The YAC creates a space where youth can: Influence real organizational programming Build national connections Strengthen advocacy skills Gain professional development experience Participate in meaningful environmental initiatives Collaborate with like-minded changemakers The programme also amplifies youth voices across national and global networks connected to the Jane Goodall Institute. Benefits of Joining the YAC Selected participants will gain access to numerous personal, professional, and leadership development opportunities. 1. Leadership Development Members will strengthen their leadership capacity through active participation in environmental and youth-focused initiatives. 2. Project Management Experience Participants will co-design and implement projects connected to sustainability, biodiversity, climate justice, and environmental equity. 3. National Networking Opportunities YAC members will connect with passionate youth leaders from various regions across Canada. 4. Professional Development Opportunities Participants gain access to workshops, mentorship opportunities, and professional growth experiences. 5. Travel Opportunities The programme offers travel opportunities for current and alumni YAC members where applicable. 6. Collaboration with a Globally Recognized Organization Participants become connected to the internationally respected Jane Goodall Institute network. 7. Youth Voice Amplification Council members contribute directly to shaping programme direction and ensuring youth perspectives are represented in organizational activities. What Participants Will Do Members of the Youth Advisory Council are expected to: Participate in meetings and collaborative sessions Contribute ideas for youth-centered programming Engage in environmental advocacy activities Work on self-directed and co-designed projects Collaborate with fellow youth leaders Support awareness and outreach initiatives Help shape the direction of Canada Programs within JGIC Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for: Youth passionate about environmental sustainability Climate advocates and environmental activists Students interested in leadership and social impact Young professionals seeking volunteer leadership experience Community organizers Biodiversity conservation advocates Individuals committed to environmental justice and equity Youth interested in global environmental issues Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the 2026/2027 Youth Advisory Council, applicants must: Age Requirement Applicants must be between 18 and 30 years old. Residency Requirement Applicants must reside in Canada for the entire duration of the term: July 2026 to June 2027 The opportunity is available regardless of immigration status, provided applicants will remain in Canada during the programme term. Passion and Commitment Applicants should demonstrate: Commitment to positive social and environmental change Interest in youth leadership Passion for sustainability and advocacy Willingness to collaborate with peers Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the 2026/2027 JGIC Youth Advisory Council is: May 22, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare thoughtful responses that reflect their passion, leadership experiences, and commitment to environmental and social impact. Application Process Applicants are required to complete the Official Application Form and provide: Full legal name Preferred name (if applicable) Birth year Pronouns Contact information Canadian city and province of residence Referral source or how they heard about the opportunity The organization may contact applicants for interviews or clarifying questions during the selection process. Notification Timeline The Jane Goodall Institute of Canada has indicated that applicants will receive updates regarding their application status on or before: June 1, 2026 Important Residency Note This opportunity is strictly limited to youth who will be physically residing in Canada for the entirety of the July 2026 – June 2027 term. Applicants who: Do not currently live in Canada permanently, or Anticipate leaving Canada during the programme period may not be invited to participate in the Youth Advisory Council. Please click HERE to learn more about the Jane Goodall Institute of Canada Youth Advisory Council, 2026/2027. Contact Information For questions regarding the Youth Advisory Council, Roots Shoots, or the Jane Goodall Institute of Canada, applicants may contact: rootsandshoots@janegoodall.ca Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Jane Goodall Institute of Canada Youth Advisory Council 2026/2027: Fully Immersive Leadership Opportunity for Young Environmental Changemakers in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
